headline: Anthropic model deployment dominated the month as the White House reversed a two-week export restriction on the Mythos and Fable models.
body: The White House imposed and then dropped restrictions on Anthropic's most advanced models, with the administration lifting export curbs on Mythos and Fable and Anthropic set to restore Fable access beginning July 1. New model releases crowded the same window: Sonnet 5 shipped but drew a review finding it underperforms Opus 4.8 and GLM-5.2 while costing more, and Fable 5 was flagged as imminent. On the technique side, a broad cluster of research methods broke out together, led by trajectory data, Gradient Staleness, agent skills, behavioral auditing, and ablation, with masking, HNSW, and long-context also entering as new movers. Enterprise agentic AI advanced via Google Cloud's FynanceAI demonstration orchestrating data across databases, BI, and vector stores through the Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol.

cited_items.title: White House drops restrictions on Anthropic AI models after two-week ban - The Washington Post
